description: Hospital-based intensive treatment protocols for severe cases

with_edema.details: Structured 3-phase approach: Stabilization (F75, 100 kcal/kg/day), Transition (F75-F100 mix, 130 kcal/kg/day), Rehabilitation (F100/RUTF, 200 kcal/kg/day). Include IV fluids if needed, strict infection control
with_edema.follow_up: 24/7 medical monitoring, daily medical rounds, weekly multidisciplinary team review

without_edema.treatment: Outpatient therapeutic program with hospital backup
without_edema.details: Intensive outpatient management with daily clinic visits initially, structured RUTF provision, immediate hospital admission criteria established
without_edema.follow_up: Daily visits week 1, every 2 days week 2-4, weekly thereafter until recovery

standard.details: Structured supplementary feeding with specialized products, regular medical monitoring, nutrition counseling with dietitian
standard.follow_up: Weekly medical check-ups, bi-weekly dietitian consultations, monthly progress review

standard.details: Comprehensive preventive care including nutrition counseling, growth monitoring, immunizations, and family education programs
standard.follow_up: Monthly growth monitoring, quarterly comprehensive assessment, annual nutritional screening

standard.details: Structured dietary assessment and individualized meal plan. Reduce caloric-density foods; increase fibre and micronutrient-rich foods. Family nutrition education and physical activity prescription.
standard.follow_up: Monthly outpatient dietitian review for 6 months; then quarterly
medications: Micronutrient screening; supplement if deficient (Vitamin D, iron, zinc)
monitoring: Blood pressure at every visit (if ≥3 years)
3-day dietary intake record reviewed at each visit
Physical activity log (active-play minutes per day)

standard.details: Comprehensive evaluation by paediatrician, registered dietitian, and clinical psychologist. Structured weight management programme with medical supervision. Screen and manage comorbidities. Rule out endocrine or genetic aetiology.
standard.follow_up: Monthly medical review; quarterly multidisciplinary team assessment
medications: No anti-obesity medications under 5 years without specialist authorisation
Treat identified comorbidities per specialist guidance
Vitamin D and micronutrient replacement as indicated by blood results
Refer to paediatric endocrinology if endocrine cause suspected
monitoring: Fasting glucose and lipid profile every 6 months if indicated
Annual developmental and psychological well-being assessment
